title: Classroom Launch Mode
subtitle: First-week coaching for routines, procedures, and classroom culture.

launchDays.goal: Students learn how to enter the classroom, unpack, gather on the carpet, and begin building classroom community.
systems: How to Sit on the Carpet
launchDays.teacherBrain: Day 1 is not about rushing curriculum. It is about teaching the room.

launchDays.goal: Students practice moving safely and respectfully through classroom and school spaces.
launchDays.teacherBrain: Practice transitions before you need them. Do not wait for chaos to teach expectations.

launchDays.goal: Students begin using routines with fewer teacher reminders.
launchDays.teacherBrain: Praise independence out loud. Students repeat what gets noticed.

launchDays.goal: Students practice listening, speaking, partner work, and materials routines during short lessons.
systems: How to Sit on the Carpet
launchDays.teacherBrain: Academic success depends on procedure success.

launchDays.goal: Students review routines, celebrate growth, and identify what still needs practice.
launchDays.teacherBrain: End Week 1 by celebrating routines that are becoming habits.

coachNotes: Teach fewer routines deeply rather than many routines quickly.
Model incorrectly, let students notice, then model correctly.
Practice routines when students are calm.
Narrate the behavior you want repeated.
Do not treat reteaching as failure. Reteaching is part of classroom culture.
